concerns.text: Task T028 (sensitivity sweep) is listed AFTER T029 (per-study JSON save). T028 explicitly states it 'reads *all* JSON files generated by T029'. This violates the producer-before-consumer rule. T028 must precede T029 in the execution order, or T029 must be split into a 'save per-study' step and a 'final aggregation' step that T028 consumes.

Task T026b (document staged exception) is placed after T026 (runtime feasibility check) and T029. Documentation of a logic constraint (T026) should logically precede or be part of the implementation of that logic, not appear as a post-hoc task after the data generation (T029) and sensitivity sweep (T028) are complete. This breaks the logical flow of 'Define Constraint -> Implement Check -> Document Exception'.

Task T027 (adaptive subsampling) is listed after T022 (bootstrap resampling) and T023 (alternative specs). T027 describes logic to 'subsample to 2000' which is a prerequisite for the resampling logic in T022/T023 to function correctly on large datasets. T027 should be a prerequisite task or integrated into T022/T023, not a subsequent step.

Task T009 (Setup directory structure with checksumming) is listed after T005-T008 (skeleton implementations). While T005-T008 are 'skeletons', T009 implies the creation of the actual data paths that T005 (ingestion) and T006 (bootstrap) will write to. T009 should precede T005-T008 to ensure the file system exists before the code attempts to write to it.
